findings.title: ImportSkillsDto.skills array has no @ArrayMaxSize — sequential DB writes enable per-request DoS
findings.description: The `skills` array in `ImportSkillsDto` is validated with `@IsArray()` and `@ValidateNested()` but has no `@ArrayMaxSize()` constraint. The global body limit is 1 MiB. A minimal valid skill entry (`{"name":"a","category":"TECHNICAL"}`) is ~35 bytes, allowing approximately 30,000 entries per request. `importSkills()` in `skills.service.ts` processes each item in a sequential `for` loop, performing one `prisma.skill.create()` call per entry (plus an initial `prisma.skill.findMany()`). `validateImport()` similarly iterates with per-item logic. Both endpoints require `skills:create` permission (legitimate users only), but any user holding that role can trigger thousands of DB writes per HTTP request.
findings.root_cause: No `@ArrayMaxSize()` decorator on `ImportSkillsDto.skills`, combined with a sequential per-item DB write loop in the service, means a single large payload causes unbounded database load.
findings.impact: An authenticated user with `skills:create` permission can submit a 1 MiB array of ~30,000 skills and cause thousands of sequential Prisma `create` calls per request, potentially saturating the database connection pool and degrading service for all other users.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@ArrayMaxSize(500)` (or a similarly small bound) from `class-validator` to the `skills` field in `ImportSkillsDto`. Also convert the sequential loop in `importSkills()` to use `prisma.skill.createMany()` or a batched `$transaction` for efficiency.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/skills/import with skills array > 500 items returns HTTP 400
POST /api/skills/import/validate with skills array > 500 items returns HTTP 400
POST /api/skills/import with exactly 500 items succeeds (if authorized)
findings.verification_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:4000/api/skills/import -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"skills":[{"name":"s","category":"TECHNICAL"}]}' | jq .  # then repeat with 501 items and expect 400
findings.notes: Both POST /skills/import and POST /skills/import/validate are affected.

findings.title: ImportSkillDto.name and .description lack @MaxLength — inconsistent with CreateSkillDto which enforces @MaxLength(100)
findings.description: The `ImportSkillDto` used for bulk import accepts `name` without `@MaxLength` (nor `@MinLength`), and `description` without `@MaxLength`. By contrast, `CreateSkillDto` enforces `@MinLength(2)` and `@MaxLength(100)` on `name`. A caller can embed a `name` of arbitrary length (up to the 1 MiB body limit) in each array element. The service-side validation in `validateImport()` does check `skillData.name.length < 2 || skillData.name.length > 100` and emits an error line — but the DTO itself is the correct place to enforce this, and the `importSkills()` path trusts the data without that same service-level check.
findings.root_cause: The `ImportSkillDto` was not aligned with `CreateSkillDto` when the bulk import feature was added; length constraints were omitted from the import DTO.
findings.impact: Very large `name` or `description` values (up to body limit) are accepted by `importSkills()` and written to the database without length validation, potentially storing oversized data that could break downstream display logic. Risk is bounded by the 1 MiB global body limit.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MinLength(2)` and `@MaxLength(100)` to `name`, and `@MaxLength(500)` to `description` in `ImportSkillDto`, matching the constraints in `CreateSkillDto`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/skills/import with a skill name > 100 chars returns 400
POST /api/skills/import/validate with a skill name < 2 chars returns an error in the response preview
findings.notes: The service-side validateImport() catches the length issue in the preview path, but importSkills() does not perform the same check.

findings.title: description fields in CreateSkillDto, CreateDepartmentDto, CreateServiceDto have no @MaxLength
findings.description: The optional `description` field in `CreateSkillDto`, `CreateDepartmentDto`, and `CreateServiceDto` has `@IsString()` and `@IsOptional()` but no `@MaxLength()`. Any authenticated user with the appropriate create/update permission can submit a description of up to 1 MiB. While the global body limit (1 MiB) provides an outer bound, there is no DTO-level constraint preventing storage of a very large description string that could degrade rendering or storage.
findings.root_cause: No `@MaxLength()` decorator was applied to optional free-text description fields in these DTOs.
findings.impact: Low direct security impact (bounded by 1 MiB body limit and requires authenticated access), but inconsistent with defence-in-depth and could facilitate large data storage per record.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MaxLength(1000)` (or a business-appropriate limit) to `description` in `CreateSkillDto`, `CreateDepartmentDto`, and `CreateServiceDto`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/skills with description > 1000 chars returns HTTP 400
POST /api/departments with description > 1000 chars returns HTTP 400
POST /api/services with description > 1000 chars returns HTTP 400
findings.notes: Same pattern in apps/api/src/departments/dto/create-department.dto.ts lines 24-31 and apps/api/src/services/dto/create-service.dto.ts lines 25-32.

findings.subcategory: input-validation / missing UUID pipe on query param
findings.title: departmentId query parameter in GET /services and GET /skills/matrix is not validated as UUID — invalid values cause DB-level errors surfaced as 500
findings.description: The `departmentId` query parameter in `GET /api/services` (and `GET /api/skills/matrix` in skills.controller.ts line 92) is accepted as a raw string without a `ParseUUIDPipe`. The value is passed directly to Prisma as a `where.departmentId` filter. If the value is not a valid UUID, the PostgreSQL driver raises an `invalid_text_representation` error (PrismaClientKnownRequestError P2023), which propagates as an HTTP 500. The global `AllExceptionsFilter` masks the stack trace in the response, but generates a 500 log entry instead of a clean 400 validation error.
findings.root_cause: Missing `ParseUUIDPipe` (with `optional: true`) on the `departmentId` query parameter in both controller methods.
findings.impact: Any authenticated user with `services:read` or `skills:manage_matrix` permission can trigger repeated 500 error log entries by passing a malformed `departmentId`. This is not exploitable for data leakage (the error is masked), but degrades observability and could obscure real errors in logs.
findings.suggested_fix: Apply `@Query('departmentId', new ParseUUIDPipe({ optional: true })) departmentId?: string` in both `ServicesController.findAll()` and `SkillsController.getMatrix()`. The same pattern is already used for path params (e.g., `@Param('departmentId', ParseUUIDPipe)`).
findings.notes: The same issue exists in apps/api/src/skills/skills.controller.ts line 92 for GET /skills/matrix?departmentId.

verified_clean: All controller route handlers in skills, departments, services, personal-todos have either @RequirePermissions or @AllowSelfService decorators — no unguarded routes
Global JwtAuthGuard, ForcePasswordChangeGuard, ThrottlerBehindProxyGuard, and PermissionsGuardV2 are all registered as APP_GUARD and apply to all routes
PermissionsGuardV2 is active (registered in RbacModule as APP_GUARD); the stale 'not registered' comment in permissions.guard.ts is outdated documentation only
No $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe calls in any scanned module — all DB queries use Prisma parameterized APIs
No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or passwords found in any scanned source file
No secret values written to logs in any scanned service
No external URL fields in any DTO — no SSRF risk
No file upload handling in any scanned module
All path param IDs use ParseUUIDPipe (departments/:id, services/:id, skills/:id, skills/user/:userId, etc.)
Personal todos ownership check: update() and delete() in personal-todos.service.ts explicitly verify todo.userId === userId before allowing mutation (prevents IDOR)
PersonalTodosService cleanupOldCompleted is always scoped to the authenticated userId — no cross-user data leakage
CreateSkillDto: name has @MinLength(2) + @MaxLength(100), category has @IsEnum(SkillCategory), requiredCount has @IsInt + @Min(1)
CreateDepartmentDto: name has @MinLength(2) + @MaxLength(100), managerId has @IsUUID
CreateServiceDto: name has @MinLength(2) + @MaxLength(100), color has @Matches(/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/), departmentId and managerId have @IsUUID
AssignSkillDto: skillId has @IsUUID, level has @IsEnum(SkillLevel)
UpdateSkillLevelDto: level has @IsEnum(SkillLevel)
CreatePersonalTodoDto: text has @MaxLength(200)
UpdatePersonalTodoDto: text has @MaxLength(200), completed has @IsBoolean
Global ValidationPipe is configured with whitelist:true and forbidNonWhitelisted:true — extra body properties are stripped/rejected
Global body limit of 1 MiB (bodyLimit: 1048576) is set on the Fastify adapter
Production boot assertions prevent RBAC permissive mode, weak JWT secret, and auth token exposure
